headline: Electricity Tariff Hike of 17% Hits Manufacturers Hard
context: Tenaga Nasional announced a 17% average tariff increase effective January 2026 under the Incentive-Based Regulation mechanism, with industrial users absorbing the largest increase at 21 sen per kWh.

cards.big: 17% electricity hike hits 850,000 commercial accounts — factories in Penang face RM2.4 million annual cost increases
cards.sub: Manufacturers warn production may shift to Vietnam and Indonesia where industrial tariffs are 30% lower

cards.big: Malaysia's industrial electricity tariff at 42 sen/kWh now exceeds Vietnam's 32 sen and Indonesia's 28 sen
cards.sub: Energy cost competitiveness gap threatens RM18 billion in export-oriented manufacturing output

cards.big: Low-income households face RM45 monthly increase despite rebate — cooking and cooling costs squeeze B40 budgets
cards.sub: 8.2 million domestic accounts affected; only 2.1 million qualify for targeted electricity subsidy

cards.big: Energy Commission approved TNB's 9.8% return on assets — higher than regional utility average of 7.2%
cards.sub: IBR mechanism guarantees TNB shareholder returns while consumers absorb fuel cost pass-through

cards.big: When TNB's guaranteed profits rise with every tariff hike, the incentive is to raise prices — not reduce costs
Should TNB's guaranteed rate of return be capped at 7% with savings passed directly to industrial users?
